_meta.region: US-federal (DEFAULT — matches the Rosa persona; team confirms US vs. India before Milestone 6)
_meta.note: 9 rules, web-confirmed against official .gov/.org sources on 2026-06-16 (see each row's source_note). 'verified: true' here means the program, the source_url, and the general eligibility basis were confirmed against the cited official page. Exact local dollar/AMI figures vary by locality and are intentionally NOT asserted — they are described in eligibility_criteria.notes. Team should spot-check the figures for the specific demo city and may add their chosen local PHA / legal-aid org to push toward ~15 rules.

categories.income_tested_benefit: engine compares the user's income_band to income_band_max -> may_qualify / likely_not / need_more_info
categories.referral_resource: a navigator/referral that connects to local help; broadly available, framed as an option not a determination
categories.universal_resource: free service with no income test (counseling, crisis shelter, legal info)

eligibility_criteria.notes: HUD: family income generally may not exceed 50% of the area median income (AMI). At least 75% of new vouchers must go to extremely-low-income (<=30% AMI) families. Waitlists are common and sometimes closed. Apply through your local PHA.

rules.plain_summary: Helps families with very low income pay rent for a home they choose in the private market. You apply through your local housing agency (PHA).
rules.source_note: Confirmed program, eligibility (<=50% AMI, citizen/eligible-immigration, PHA-administered) and URL against HUD.gov, 2026-06-16.
rules.team_todo: Confirm the AMI dollar threshold for your demo city's metro area.

eligibility_criteria.notes: HUD: decent, safe rental housing for eligible low-income families, the elderly, and persons with disabilities; rent is income-based. Income limits are set locally by the PHA within HUD limits (generally <=80% AMI, with priority to lower bands).

rules.plain_summary: Low-rent apartments owned by the government for people with low income, seniors, and people with disabilities. You apply at your local housing agency.
rules.source_note: Confirmed program, eligibility category and URL against HUD.gov, 2026-06-16.
rules.team_todo: Confirm local income limits with the demo city's PHA.

rules.program: Privately Owned Subsidized Housing (HUD Multifamily / Project-Based Section 8, 202, 811)

eligibility_criteria.administered_by: private property owners under HUD contract
eligibility_criteria.notes: HUD pays private apartment owners so rent is reduced for low-income tenants; rent is based on income. Includes Project-Based Rental Assistance, Section 202 (elderly) and Section 811 (disabilities). You apply directly at the property's management office; use HUD's property search to find participating buildings.

rules.plain_summary: Privately owned apartments where the government lowers your rent based on your income. You apply directly at the apartment office; HUD's online search finds buildings near you.
rules.source_note: Confirmed program descriptions (PBRA/202/811) and property-search URL against HUD.gov, 2026-06-16.

eligibility_criteria.notes: Dial 211 (or 211.org) to be connected to local emergency rental assistance, eviction-prevention funds, and legal aid. Availability and income rules vary by locality. This is information and referral, not legal advice.

rules.plain_summary: If you are facing eviction now, dial 2-1-1 (free, confidential) to be connected to local emergency rent help and eviction-prevention programs in your area.
rules.source_note: Confirmed 211 housing-expenses referral page and scope against 211.org, 2026-06-16. (Note: Treasury's pandemic-era ERA program has largely wound down; 211 is the durable entry point to current local programs.)

rules.program: Find Help With Rent and Bills (CFPB renter tool)

eligibility_criteria.notes: Official federal hub that helps renters find state/local rental and utility assistance, understand protections, and connect to housing counselors. No income test to use the tool; underlying programs have their own rules.

rules.plain_summary: An official government tool that helps you find rent and utility help in your area and explains your options as a renter.
rules.source_note: Confirmed CFPB renter help page and URL against consumerfinance.gov, 2026-06-16.

rules.program: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)

eligibility_criteria.notes: Helps pay heating/cooling bills and provides emergency help during an energy crisis. Income limits vary by state (commonly up to 150% of the federal poverty level or 60% of state median income). Apply via EnergyHelp.us or the NEAR hotline 1-866-674-6327. LIHEAP never charges a fee.

rules.plain_summary: Helps pay your heating or cooling bills, and can give emergency help if your power is about to be shut off. Apply through your state's energy assistance office.
rules.source_note: Confirmed program, eligibility basis, NEAR hotline and URL against acf.hhs.gov, 2026-06-16.
rules.team_todo: Confirm the income threshold used by the demo state.

rules.program: Free Legal Aid for Eviction & Tenant Rights (LawHelp.org / Legal Services Corporation)

eligibility_criteria.administered_by: LSC-funded nonprofit legal aid programs (130 nationwide)
eligibility_criteria.notes: LawHelp.org gives free self-help info and finds local legal aid in every state; anyone can use the information. Free legal REPRESENTATION from LSC-funded programs is income-tested at <=125% of the federal poverty guidelines (2025: $19,563 for an individual, $40,188 for a family of four). This is information, not legal advice.

rules.plain_summary: Free help understanding your rights and, if you qualify by income, a free lawyer for your eviction. Find local legal aid through LawHelp.org.
rules.source_note: Confirmed LawHelp/LSC scope and the 125% FPL representation threshold ($19,563 / $40,188, 2025) against lawhelp.org and lsc.gov, 2026-06-16.
rules.team_todo: Optionally add the specific LSC-funded legal-aid org for your demo city as its own rule.

eligibility_criteria.notes: Free or low-cost counseling to help renters understand options, build a plan, and avoid eviction. No income test. Find a counselor at HUD's site or call 800-569-4287 (TTY 202-708-1455).

rules.plain_summary: Talk to a trained housing counselor for free to understand your options and make a plan. Call 800-569-4287 to find one near you.
rules.source_note: Confirmed HUD housing-counseling service, phone number and URL against HUD.gov, 2026-06-16.

rules.program: HUD Find Shelter (emergency housing & crisis resources)

eligibility_criteria.notes: Searchable directory of nearby shelter, housing, health care, and clothing resources for people at risk of or experiencing homelessness. No income test to search. A safety-net fallback when eviction is imminent.

rules.plain_summary: If you might lose your home very soon, this official tool finds emergency shelter and help near you.
rules.source_note: Confirmed HUD Find Shelter tool and URL against HUD.gov, 2026-06-16.
